title: andinocleome lechleri
intro: Andinocleome lechleri, also known as Andina or Mayan Gold, is a plant species in the bignon family, native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antimicrobial properties. Andinocleome lechleri is believed to have several health benefits, including the ability to reduce inflammation, improve wound healing, and boost the immune system. The plant is often used in herbal preparations such as capsules, tinctures, and creams, which can be applied topically or taken orally to reap its benefits.
